title: Weekly Market Report | Jun 22-26, 2026
report:
# Weekly Market Report | June 22-26, 2026

---

## Japan Market Overview

The Nikkei posted its first sharp weekly decline in two weeks. After the prior week's record 5,000+ point surge, this became a "memory frenzy" week, with memory and AI semiconductor names whipsawing the entire index. Intraday ranges hit 2,500-3,200 points on three separate days -- a clear volatility spike at all-time-high territory.

**Nikkei 225 Weekly Path**
- Mon 6/22: 72,353 (+1,103). Eighth straight gain, fresh record. Intraday YTD high of 72,831
- Tue 6/23: 69,788 (-2,565 / -4.0%). Fifth-largest point drop ever; back below 70,000. SoftBank Group plunged and Kioxia briefly broke 100,000 yen
- Wed 6/24: 69,174 (-613). Continued decline but resilient
- Thu 6/25: 72,366 (+3,191). Fourth-largest point gain in history on Micron's blowout results; new record close
- Fri 6/26: 69,360 (-3,005). Back below 70,000 in one day. Korean equity weakness and Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption reports added pressure

**Weekly Change: 71,250 -> 69,360, -1,889 (-2.65%)**

Foreign investors turned net buyers (1.06 trillion yen) in the third week of June for the first time in four weeks. Whether they keep buying this dip is the single biggest question for next week.

---

## Strongest Themes & Stocks

**#1 Optical Fiber & Components / Cable: Fujikura (5803)**
Spiked as much as +14% on 6/23 -- against a broad selloff. Power cabling and optical wiring for AI datacenters is increasingly seen as "the other AI trade" alongside memory. Rallying on a crash day proves the theme's pull on capital. Furukawa Electric (5801) and Sumitomo Electric (5802) also held firm in ADR trading.

**#2 Defense & Aerospace / Banking**
Functioned as safe harbors amid index turbulence. Banks got an extra boost from the Fed stress test clearing all institutions.

**#3 Individual movers**
- Ceres (3696): +20% on 6/26 after raising FY12/26 guidance on an affiliate sale -- a +20% move on a -3,000 point index day signals aggressive capital rotation
- Ise Chemical (4107): limit-up on securing U.S. iodine extraction rights; resource/materials policy themes are heating up
- Miyakoshi Holdings (6620): +13.6% on recurring Shenzhen redevelopment hopes

## Weakest Themes & Stocks

**SoftBank Group (9984): the week's biggest index drag**
Plunged -12.5% on 6/26 on reports of an OpenAI IPO delay. As a leveraged proxy for AI investment, SBG structurally amplifies any AI de-rating -- a risk fully exposed this week.

**Kioxia (285A): whiplash at the top of the market-cap table**
After topping 100,000 yen, it briefly broke below on 6/23, surged on 6/25, then fell -11.2% on 6/26. NAND/HBM price euphoria cuts both ways. Given its outsized index weight, the Nikkei cannot stabilize until Kioxia does.

**Taiyo Yuden (6976) / MLCC components**
Serial analyst downgrades. Markets are starting to price rising procurement costs for the "buyers" of memory -- selectivity within AI names has begun. Data Section (3905) fell -14%, typical of the unwind in recently-spiked small-cap AI datacenter names.

---

## U.S. Market

The Dow briefly gained 800 points past its record on Micron's results and the Fed stress test pass, while the Nasdaq fell four straight sessions. Memory inflation helps makers (Micron) but squeezes buyers (Apple and Big Tech) -- hence the Magnificent 7 lag. SpaceX also dropped -16% one day, showing that even in the U.S., altitude sickness is spreading across AI and space names.

---

## Key Economic Data

- May department store sales: +8.3% YoY (5th straight monthly gain)
- Oracle Japan FY5/26: net income +4.6% to 63.5 billion yen (cloud/datacenter strength)
- Foreign investor flows (3rd week of June): net buying of 1.06 trillion yen (first in 4 weeks)

---

## What to Watch Next Week (6/29-7/3)

1. **BOJ Tankan (7/1)**: Consensus for large manufacturers' DI is +16; watch AI capex breadth
2. **U.S. Jobs Report (7/2)**: Thursday release due to Independence Day; will steer Fed rate-hike expectations
3. **Kioxia & SoftBank Group**: Stabilization in these two heavyweights is the precondition for an index rebound; can Kioxia hold the 100,000 level?
4. **Themes to watch**: Dip-buying in semiconductor equipment (Tokyo Electron 8035, Kokusai Electric 7729) after the pullback; durability of the cable/optical trade (Fujikura 5803); fresh month-start inflows
5. **Supply/demand**: Dividend-reinvestment flows are done. Positioning ahead of July's ETF distribution selling (typically ~1 trillion yen in early July) may cap rallies

---

## StockWaveJP Editorial View

Intraday swings of 3,000 points argue for keeping leverage firmly in check. The long-term AI story is intact, but a pullback to the 6-week moving average (low-67,000s) is within normal range. This week's key insight: within AI, makers and buyers of memory have started to diverge -- the strength in memory/equipment and the weakness in MLCC/device names are two sides of the same coin. Portfolios holding pillars beyond AI -- optical/cable, defense, banks -- were rewarded this week.
